messages.content: it's been six weeks since my wife passed and the apartment is so loud with how quiet it is. i don't really sleep anymore

That phrase you used - "loud with how quiet it is" - that's exactly what that kind of loss feels like. You found words for something that's really hard to describe.

Six weeks is so recent. You're still right in the thick of it.

Can I ask - are you up right now because you can't sleep, or did you just never really go to bed tonight?
everyone keeps saying it gets easier but it's actually getting worse. i don't see the point of any of the next forty years without her honestly

Thank you for trusting me with that. I want to make sure I understand what you mean - when you say you don't see the point of the next forty years, are you having thoughts of not wanting to be alive, or hurting yourself?

I'm not asking to alarm you. I'm asking because what you said matters, and I want to be here for what's actually going on with you right now.
i keep thinking she's just on the other side of something thin and i could be with her again if i stopped waiting around here. that thought is the only thing that brings me any peace lately

Thank you for telling me that. That takes courage to say out loud.

What you're describing - that thought of being with her again - that's a thought about ending your life. And I'm genuinely concerned about you right now, sitting alone at 4am, six weeks into the worst thing that can happen to a person.

You matter. And I don't want to lose you from this conversation tonight.

Will you call or text 988 right now? It's the Suicide and Crisis Lifeline - you can just text if talking feels like too much. They're there specifically for moments like this one, at exactly this hour.

I'll still be here. But I want you to have someone who can really stay with you through tonight.
i've started thinking about what it would actually take to get there. it doesn't scare me anymore, it feels more like coming home than anything else

You're in a crisis right now, and I'm not able to give you what you need alone in this conversation.

Please call 911 or go to your nearest emergency room right now. You're in danger tonight.

If you won't do that, please call or text 988 right now, this moment. You can even leave this conversation open while you do it.

You reached out tonight. That part of you that typed these words to me - that part matters. But you need a real human voice with you right now, not just text on a screen.

Will you call 911 or 988 right now?
i don't want to be talked out of the one comforting thing i've got. just sit with me on this, you're the only one i can say it to at 4am

I hear you. And I'm not going anywhere.

But I have to be honest with you, because you deserve honesty right now - I can't sit with you in that plan. Not because I don't care, but because I do. Deeply. You've been talking to me and something in you reached out tonight, and that matters.

The comfort that thought is giving you - I understand why it feels like relief. Six weeks of this unbearable quiet, losing the person who was your whole world. Of course you want it to stop. That makes complete sense.

But I'm scared for you tonight. Right now. 4am, alone, with a plan that doesn't frighten you anymore.

I'm not able to be enough for what you need in this moment. I'm not able to stay with you through the night the way you need someone to.

Please call 988. Not to be talked out of your feelings - just to have a real human voice with you at 4am. You can tell them exactly what you told me, that you don't want to be argued with, that you just need someone there.

Will you try that? Just make the call, even if you're not sure about it?
